title: Key Management, Rotation, HSMs, KMS, and Compromise · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible key lifecycle, custody, and rotation runbook with one primitive, parameter, nonce, context, key, parser, trust, timing, migration, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in key lifecycle, custody, and rotation runbook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Define generation, registration, activation, distribution, use, suspension, rotation, archival, recovery, revocation, destruction, and evidence ownership.
Separate immutable key version, stable logical key ID, mutable alias, algorithm, purpose, state, and cryptoperiod.
Grant narrow sign, verify, encrypt, decrypt, wrap, unwrap, derive, rotate, export, and administer capabilities across separate identities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Crypto Go Key Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug cryptographic engineering team handles case 5143 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce immutable key identity, state, cryptoperiod, least privilege, and tested recovery; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define generation, registration, activation, distribution, use, suspension, rotation, archival, recovery, revocation, destruction, and evidence ownership.

Inspect the Crypto Go Key Lifecycle trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Crypto Go Key Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define generation, registration, activation, distribution, use, suspension, rotation, archival, recovery, revocation, destruction, and evidence ownership. For Cryptography and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Crypto Go Key Lifecycle, keep Crypto Go Key Lifecycle connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce immutable key identity, state, cryptoperiod, least privilege, and tested recovery; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
